content: I just got back from a weekend trip to LA and I'm feeling so inspired to cook new dishes. I tried this amazing sourdough bread at a bakery in Silverlake and I need to recreate it.
That sounds delicious! Sourdough can be a bit tricky to make, but with the right recipe and starter, you can achieve that perfect crust. What kind of recipes have you tried so far?
I've tried a few, but I think I need to work on my starter. I've been using a recipe from King Arthur Flour, but I'm not sure if it's the right one for San Diego's climate. Do you have any tips?

Yes, climate can definitely affect your sourdough starter. You might want to try adjusting the temperature and humidity levels in your kitchen. Also, have you considered joining a local baking community or taking a class to learn more about sourdough?

That's a great idea! I'll look into it. I've been meaning to check out the San Diego Baking Club anyway. Maybe I'll meet some fellow bakers who can give me some tips. My roommate, Alex, is also really into baking, so I'll have to get her involved too.
